warnings: PTArcade preferred region has v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV, much lower than original model range [100, 1e6] GeV. This changes mass scale of all particles.
Dark gauge bosons have masses 0.28-0.64 GeV in PTA-preferred region, making them accessible to beam-dump and collider constraints.
Strong supercooling regime (alpha >> 0.5) may affect applicability of some cosmological constraints.
No SM portal couplings included in model - dark sector is secluded, limiting direct production constraints.
approximations: High-temperature expansion for thermal potential may break down in strong supercooling regime
No Daisy resummation for thermal masses

v.description: Vacuum expectation value of scalar doublet

m_W.description: Mass of dark gauge bosons W±

beta_lambda.description: CW beta function for quartic coupling

notes: Region extracted from ptarcade_bayes.json 1-sigma credible intervals
Strong supercooling regime: T_* << v, alpha >> 0.5 (up to 10^16)
Model assumes xi=1 (thermal equilibrium with SM)
No portal couplings to SM - dark sector is secluded

constraints.name: Beam dump constraints on sub-GeV dark gauge bosons
constraints.description: Beam dump experiments (E137, E141, E774, LSND, MiniBooNE) constrain dark gauge bosons with kinetic mixing that decay visibly or invisibly.
constraints.observable: Production and decay of dark gauge bosons via kinetic mixing
constraints.applicability: NOT_APPLICABLE - Model has no kinetic mixing (epsilon=0). Beam dump constraints require portal coupling.
applicability_notes: Model has no SM portal couplings (epsilon=0, lambda_HPhi=0) as per assumptions
Without kinetic mixing or Higgs portal, dark gauge bosons cannot be produced at beam dumps
If portal couplings are added, beam dump constraints would become relevant for m_W ~ 0.3-0.6 GeV

source.title: Review of beam dump constraints on light dark sectors

constraints.name: Collider mono-X constraints on sub-GeV states
constraints.description: LHC mono-X searches (jet+MET, photon+MET) constrain production of dark sector states that decay invisibly.
constraints.observable: Invisible decay of dark sector particles
constraints.applicability: NOT_APPLICABLE - No portal couplings in model
applicability_notes: Model assumes no portal interactions (lambda_HPhi=0, epsilon=0)
Without production mechanism via SM, collider constraints do not apply
If Higgs portal is added, mono-Higgs or VBF+MET searches could constrain

source.title: LHC constraints on dark matter and dark sectors

constraints.name: Fixed-target electron scattering (e.g., APEX, DarkLight)
constraints.description: Electron beam fixed-target experiments search for dark photons and dark gauge bosons via electron scattering.
constraints.observable: Electron scattering cross section via kinetic mixing
applicability_notes: Requires kinetic mixing epsilon > 0 for electron coupling
Model has epsilon=0 by assumption

source.title: Dark photon searches at fixed-target experiments

constraints.name: Vector dark matter relic abundance
constraints.description: SU(2)_D gauge bosons (W±, W³) can be vector dark matter candidates. Their relic abundance must not exceed observed DM density.
missing_observables: annihilation cross section for W+ W- -> scalars
constraints.applicability: APPROXIMATELY_TESTABLE - Literature (2109.11558) discusses non-thermal production mechanism

tested_subregion.notes: In original model range (v ~ 100-1000 GeV), vector DM relic abundance is viable. In PTA-preferred region (v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV), masses are too low for standard freeze-out but non-thermal production from SM bath via scalar portal may still work.

applicability_notes: Literature (2109.11558) finds viable non-thermal vector DM for v ~ 100-10000 GeV
At v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV, m_W ~ 0.3-0.6 GeV, standard thermal freeze-out unlikely
Non-thermal production from SM bath requires portal coupling (neglected in model)
Custodial SO(3) symmetry (from SSB pattern) may stabilize vector DM

source.title: A first order dark SU(2)_D phase transition with vector dark matter in the light of NANOGrav 12.5 yr data

constraints.name: Dark matter self-interaction constraints
constraints.description: Self-interacting dark matter constraints from galactic and cluster scales limit cross sections.
required_observables: W+ W- -> W+ W- scattering cross section
W W -> rho rho cross section
constraints.applicability: REQUIRES_NEW_BACKEND - Need to compute dark sector scattering
applicability_notes: Vector DM self-interaction via gauge coupling g_D
Cross section ~ g_D^4 / m_W^2 ~ 10^{-2} to 1 cm^2/g for PTA region
Galactic constraints: sigma/m < 0.1 cm^2/g (galactic halos)
Cluster constraints: sigma/m < 1 cm^2/g
May be relevant for m_W ~ 0.3-0.6 GeV

constraints.name: BBN constraints on light scalar decays
constraints.description: Light scalars (m_rho ~ 36-86 MeV, m_G ~ 18-43 MeV) decaying during or after BBN can alter light element abundances.

tested_subregion.notes: All PTA-preferred region has m_rho, m_G < 100 MeV

applicability_notes: m_rho ~ 36-86 MeV, m_G ~ 18-43 MeV in PTA-preferred region
BBN constraints apply for lifetimes > 1 second or electromagnetic/strong decays
Without portal couplings to SM, scalars are stable on cosmological timescales
If gravitationally coupled only, decays are negligible
But model may need to check if scalars contribute to N_eff or dark radiation

constraints.name: N_eff constraints from CMB
constraints.description: Additional relativistic degrees of freedom during CMB epoch contribute to N_eff.
constraints.observable: Effective number of neutrino species N_eff
applicability_notes: Model assumes xi=1 (thermal equilibrium with SM)
But if dark sector decouples before QCD, xi may differ
FOPT at T_* << v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV, so after QCD epoch
Dark sector particles with m < T_CMB ~ 10^-4 GeV would contribute to N_eff
In PTA region, all dark particles have m > 18 MeV, so non-relativistic at CMB
But if T_reh > T_CMB, dark sector may be reheated and contribute

source.title: Planck 2018 results. X. Constraints on inflation

constraints.name: Structure formation constraints from free-streaming
constraints.description: Light dark matter candidates with large free-streaming length can suppress structure formation.

tested_subregion.notes: m_W ~ 0.3-0.6 GeV corresponds to warm DM regime

applicability_notes: m_W ~ 0.3-0.6 GeV corresponds to warm dark matter
Free-streaming length ~ 100 kpc to 1 Mpc for these masses
Lyman-alpha forest constraints: m_W > 1-10 keV for cold DM (well satisfied)
Warm DM constraints: m_W > 1-5 keV (well satisfied for m_W ~ 300-600 MeV)
No strong structure formation constraints for these masses

source.title: Structure formation with warm dark matter

constraints.name: Supernova cooling constraints (SN1987A)
constraints.description: New light particles produced in supernova core can carry away energy, altering neutrino burst.
applicability_notes: SN1987A constraints require production via SM interactions
Model has no portal couplings (epsilon=0, lambda_HPhi=0)
Dark sector particles cannot be produced in SN core
If produced via kinetic mixing, constraints would be severe for m < 100 MeV

constraints.name: Stellar cooling constraints (horizontal branch, red giants)
constraints.description: Light particles produced in stellar interiors can carry away energy, affecting stellar evolution.
applicability_notes: Stellar constraints require production via electromagnetic or weak interactions
Model has no portal couplings to SM gauge bosons or fermions
Without portal, dark sector particles cannot be produced in stars

source.title: Stellar cooling constraints on new physics

constraints.name: Collider exotics searches (e.g., LEP, BaBar)
constraints.description: e+e- colliders search for new particles in final states.
constraints.applicability: REQUIRES_RECAST - Need portal coupling for production
applicability_notes: LEP, BaBar searched for light particles in e+e- collisions
Without portal coupling to photons or Z, no production mechanism
With kinetic mixing epsilon, LEP constrains epsilon > 10^-2 for m < 1 GeV

constraints.name: Coleman-Weinberg validity and perturbation theory
constraints.description: Coleman-Weinberg mechanism and perturbativity of gauge coupling.
constraints.applicability: DIRECTLY_TESTABLE - Can compute from model parameters

tested_subregion.notes: beta_lambda > 0 ensures CW mechanism works. Loop expansion parameter ~ g_D^2/(4pi) ~ 0.07 for g_D ~ 0.95, which is perturbative.

applicability_notes: g_D ~ 0.91-0.98 is perturbative (g_D^2/4pi ~ 0.07)
beta_lambda = 3*g_D^4/(64*pi^2) ~ 0.003-0.004 > 0, valid for CW
Model assumptions include perturbativity: g_D < 4*pi ~ 12.56
PTA region well within perturbative regime

constraints.name: Strong supercooling and high-T expansion validity
constraints.description: Validity of high-temperature expansion in strongly supercooled FOPT.

tested_subregion.notes: alpha >> 0.5 in PTA region, high-T expansion may be inaccurate

applicability_notes: PTA-preferred region has alpha >> 0.5 (up to 10^16)
High-temperature expansion assumes T ~ v, but T_* << v in supercooling
Literature (2312.12413) notes challenges in EFT description for strong supercooling
3D EFT (dimensional reduction) may be more accurate but not implemented in backend

source.title: Gravitational waves from supercooled phase transitions: dimensional transmutation meets dimensional reduction

constraints.name: GW spectrum template validity (dbf template)
constraints.description: Validity of dissipative bulk flow (dbf) template for strongly supercooled FOPT.
constraints.applicability: DIRECTLY_TESTABLE - Template selected for large alpha

tested_subregion.notes: dbf template validated for alpha >> 0.5, which matches PTA region

applicability_notes: PTA region has alpha >> 0.5 (up to 10^16)
dbf (dissipative bulk flow) template from 2511.15687 validated for large alpha
higgsless template only valid for alpha <= 0.5, inappropriate here
bf template also valid but dbf preferred for supercooled transitions
Backend uses fixed v_w=1.0, cs=1/sqrt(3) - may need validation

source.title: GW spectrum templates for strongly supercooled phase transitions

constraints.name: Hubble rate during FOPT (eta problem)
constraints.description: Consistency of Hubble rate calculation during FOPT, especially with strong supercooling.
missing_observables: g_star(T) for dark sector with xi=1

tested_subregion.notes: T_* ~ 3-5 MeV in PTA region (from fopt_benchmarks)

applicability_notes: Model assumes xi=1 (T_D = T_SM)
With strong supercooling, T_* can be much lower than v
From fopt_benchmarks: T_* ~ 3-5 MeV for v ~ 0.6-1.3 GeV
g_star(T_* ~ MeV) ~ 10-20, need to verify in backend calculation

constraints.name: Eternal inflation constraint
constraints.description: FOPT must complete (percolate) before eternal inflation begins.

tested_subregion.notes: 50% of scan points failed due to eternal inflation in fopt_model_scan.py

applicability_notes: Backend uses Gaussian approximation for false vacuum fraction and percolation condition
200 out of 400 scan points failed due to eternal inflation
PTA-preferred region points (from benchmark scan) all marked as 'viable'
Need to verify percolation condition holds in PTA 1-sigma region

source.title: Percolation condition for first-order phase transitions

missing_calculations.observable: Scalar decay widths and branching ratios
Dark sector thermal history and decoupling
Bubble wall velocity and sound speed
